# Relationship Between Amount of Students Work (online) and Desire for Livestream option for class

<!-- This is a comment. Below, you'll see code for inserting an image. To make this image appear, update <custom-path>. To add an image, save it inside the imgs folder of this repository. -->
<img src="comp110analysis/static/imgs/plot.png" alt="Analysis Boxplot "  width="500"/>

## Results
This visualization shows that students with the highest online workload of 10+ hours are most consistent in their demand for livestreaming with a medial score of 7. Students among the other groups have a much more spread out range of opinions, however generally the desire for livestream option is very apparent. There is general interest across the board in a hybrid option for the course. 
## Conclusions
I reccommend that COMP110 offer a synchronous livestreaming option for lectures. This would directly benefit students balancing heavy workloads or schedules by providing the flexibility that they clearly value. Data indicates that students with the highest external workloads, those spending 10+ hours a week working/studying online, are the most consistent in their demand for this feature with a median interest score of 7 (Strongly Agree to add livestream). Students with lighter workloads showed more varied opinion, but the overall trend suggests that livestreaming is important to students across the board. While this change creates value for students, a potential downside is a decrease in in-person attendance which can have effects on students and the instructional team. Setting up a reliable livestream everyday also requires additional technical setup (i.e. more things for the instructional team to deal with). To further validate the want for livestreaming, future collection could include questions specifically asking specifically why a student might prefer/want a livestreamed option to better tailor the hybrid experience.
